title: lapsana communis subsp. macrocarpa
intro:
Lapsana communis subsp. macrocarpa, also known as the large-fruited navelwort or large-fruited monk's head, is a subspecies of the Lapsana communis plant, a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family.

It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and as a diuretic, due to its high levels of inulin, a type of fructan that can help regulate bowel movements and reduce inflammation.

The health benefits of Lapsana communis subsp. macrocarpa include its ability to aid in weight loss, improve blood sugar control, and reduce symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), making it a popular choice among herbalists and natural health practitioners.

The plant can be prepared into various herbal products, including teas, capsules, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
